name: Ricotta and Spinach Gnocchi
intro: ‘Nudi’ in Italian means ‘naked’. They are called this because they are essentially a ravioli filling but without the pasta.

ingredients.item: .25 kg frozen whole baby spinach (not chopped), thawed
organic egg yolks and 2 whole organic eggs, beaten together
grated parmigiano, plus extra 100 g for the table

method: Squeeze all the water out of the spinach (this is very important) and chop it roughly. With a fork thoroughly whisk the ricotta, eggs, 150 g parmigiano, salt and pepper, then mix in the spinach. Take about 1 tablespoon of this mixture at a time and roll it into little balls, dusting with flour.

Boil some salted water, turn the heat down and drop about 5 balls at a time into the water. As soon as they float to the surface, they are ready (about 1 minute). With a small sieve, fish them out and put them on a heated serving dish, in a single layer. Sprinkle a generous amount of parmigiano over the nudi.
Melt the butter with the sage. When it foams, pour it over the nudi and serve immediately.
